title: Candidate-set guided prompt evidence routing

queries: G-Retriever graph question answering relevant subgraph retrieval graph prompt
GraphRAG graph-guided retrieval candidate evidence selection structured data

papers.title: G-Retriever: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Graph Question Answering
papers.claim: Retrieving task-relevant graph structure can reduce information loss compared with relying on a single compressed graph representation.
papers.relevance: Stage 3 scorer replacements repeatedly failed to preserve salt-side candidate ranking. This motivates moving candidate information earlier, into which schema/neighborhood evidence is routed into graph prompt tokens.

papers.title: Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Survey
papers.claim: GraphRAG systems organize improvements around graph indexing, graph-guided retrieval, and graph-enhanced generation.
papers.relevance: The recent Stage 3 line has over-weighted graph-enhanced candidate scoring. A graph-guided evidence-routing candidate is a different information path while keeping the frozen LLM and finetune-only protocol.

evidence.why_now: The post-EXP099 scalable scorer line has repeatedly failed: token-stat and pooled-label families collapsed, baseline residuals softened but did not solve the salt-side failure, naive late interaction and Poly-encoder variants stayed far below the strict baseline, and shared-prefix implementation rewrites either lost score-surface behavior or were not acceptable as final paths. The next non-near-duplicate Stage4-compatible move is to stop only changing the final scorer and instead make the graph prompt evidence candidate-aware before the frozen LLM consumes it.

hypothesis: For autocomplete multiclass tasks, the current prompt may route generic seed/neighborhood evidence that is useful for Amazon controls but insufficient for salt-side candidate discrimination. A candidate-set guided prompt routing mechanism should score available prompt/neighborhood tokens against the candidate label set, keep a small candidate-relevant evidence subset, and feed the existing scorer a prompt with more discriminative relational evidence. This remains Stage 3 finetune-only and Stage4-compatible because candidate-set relevance can later be trained from proxy categorical-value ranking episodes, without target-database-only fixed heads or exhaustive O(L*C) autoregressive rollout.
notes: Implementation added on 2026-05-11: prompt_candidate_guided_routing builds a compact candidate-set query from class-id and raw-label text embeddings, then reorders existing graph prompt evidence groups before LLM encoding without prompt-length inflation or scorer changes.
Keep Stage 3 finetune-only; do not use --pretrain.
This is intentionally not another scorer near-variant: it changes the information path before prompt consumption by routing graph evidence according to the candidate set.
Do not scan routing top-k or token budgets manually. Add any necessary knob for future Optuna only after the mechanism earns budget.

Implementation sketch: build a compact candidate-set query from class-id/raw-label text embeddings, compute relevance for existing graph prompt tokens or token metadata groups, and reorder or gate prompt evidence before LLM encoding while preserving batch-equivalent screening protocol.
The implementation must stay non-O(L*C) in LLM rollout; candidate text embedding and token routing should be tensorized and cheap relative to one prompt forward.
Pre-launch validation completed on 2026-05-11: py_compile, git diff --check, JSON validation, and remote item-incoterms shape/throughput sanity check all passed.

Remote sanity check completed on 2026-05-11: a 64-step item-incoterms finetune-only run on lab25211 GPU7 completed cleanly, reached roughly 6.1 train step/s with batch_size=4 (about 24.4 effective train items/sec), and eval/test ran around 12.3 it/s with val_size=1.
Exclude GPU0 for this candidate only because recent EXP162 evidence showed a pre-eval SIGTERM when user-churn used GPU0 as local_rank 0; global pipeline_config keeps gpu_exclude empty.
If implementation devolves into prompt-length inflation, scorer residual scaling, token-stat near-neighbor features, or batching/chunk-size tuning, retire this draft rather than launch it.
Final metrics: EXP180 user-churn roc_auc=0.6846461354497603; EXP181 user-ltv mae=79.0516936504784; EXP182 item-incoterms mrr=0.7432317302922772.
Final verdict under replay-aware effective deltas: user-churn better, user-ltv neutral, item-incoterms worse; global verdict failed.
Throughput was acceptable: EXP182 ran on four ranks with per-rank batch 1 at roughly 6.8 train step/s, about 27.2 effective train items/sec, and final test ran around 12.16 it/s.

Interpretation: candidate-set prompt routing improved salt-side ranking over the repeated 0.6786/0.699/0.704 collapse bands and stayed Stage4-compatible, so it is a fixed-screen failed but retune-plausible mechanism signal; it is not promotable and should not be followed by manual routing/token-budget scans.
